discussion_posts.name: njbrown180
discussion_posts.content: It may be lost on some, because it was certainly lost on me initially, but the arrays are `strictly increasing`, i.e. there are no duplicate elements in any array.

discussion_posts.name: 0p3r4t0r
discussion_posts.content: I\'m sure we all feel sometimes that leetcode problems are total trivia and a waste of time, but I really think this is a great problem. There are many ways to solve it, and if you can come up with all the different ways, it demonstrates a lot of algorithm knowledge.

discussion_posts.name: mshoosterman
discussion_posts.content:
(This comment contains minor spoilers)

Super easy solution: 
Combine into 1 array, sort it, and find all elements which occur 3 times. 

normal solution: 
3 pointer. 

Harder solution: 
clean 3 pointer. 

id highly recommend after writing a 3 pointer solution, to try and clean up your code to make it as clean as you can. Try to see if there is a straightforward way of doing it without any nested if statements.
